trade.summary: The US is Japan's largest export market and second-largest trading partner overall. Japan runs a persistent trade surplus with the US, which has historically been a source of friction. Major Japanese exports include automobiles (Toyota, Honda, Subaru), electronics, and machinery. US exports to Japan include aircraft, agricultural products (beef, soybeans, wheat), and LNG. Both are members of CPTPP (US withdrew in 2017 but relations remain strong). A 2019 US-Japan Trade Agreement reduced some tariffs.
disputes: persistent US trade deficit with Japan in automobiles historically contentious
US pressure on Japan to open agricultural markets
US concerns about Japanese currency management

diplomatic.summary: The US-Japan alliance is the cornerstone of the Asia-Pacific security order and one of the most important bilateral relationships in the world. Formalized by the 1951 Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security, the alliance includes ~54,000 US troops stationed in Japan. The relationship transformed from post-WWII occupation into a genuine strategic partnership. Japan's 2022 National Security Strategy represents a historic shift, doubling defense spending to 2% of GDP and acquiring counterstrike capabilities, all in close coordination with Washington.
recent_incidents: Japan's 2022 NSS — historic defense expansion with US backing
ongoing negotiations over host nation support costs

military.summary: The US-Japan security alliance is one of the world's most capable. The US stations 54,000 troops in Japan, operating bases in Okinawa, Yokosuka, Misawa, and elsewhere. Japan and the US conduct extensive joint exercises, share intelligence through Five Eyes-adjacent mechanisms, and are integrating their command structures. Japan's acquisition of Tomahawk cruise missiles from the US represents the deepest yet integration of offensive capabilities. The alliance is explicitly designed to deter China and North Korea.
military.alliance_status: Treaty allies — Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security (1951, revised 1960)
incidents: Okinawa basing issues remain politically sensitive in Japan
US base crimes create periodic domestic political friction

financial.summary: Japan is the largest foreign holder of US Treasury securities (~$1.1 trillion), making the two countries deeply financially intertwined. Japanese FDI in the US is among the world's largest, spanning automotive manufacturing (Toyota, Honda plants across the South and Midwest), electronics, finance, and real estate. US firms are among the largest foreign investors in Japan. The bilateral investment relationship is the world's most extensive.

energy.summary: The US has become Japan's largest LNG supplier following Russia sanctions and deliberate diversification. US LNG exports (from Gulf Coast terminals) to Japan have grown dramatically. US oil also flows to Japan. The energy relationship has deepened alongside the security alliance as Japan deliberately reduced dependence on Middle Eastern and Russian energy.

scientific.summary: The US-Japan science and technology relationship is among the most productive in the world. Joint research spans semiconductor technology, life sciences, space exploration (JAXA-NASA cooperation on Artemis lunar program), AI, quantum computing, and climate science. Technology transfer is extensive. Both countries co-chair major scientific initiatives and share classified research on defense technologies.
